incorrects.description: Student selected 11 — only counted Aiden's books, not the total

steps.text: You counted Aiden's books. We need both James AND Aiden — count James's books too.

incorrects.description: Student selected 17 — likely miscounted one or both categories

steps.text: Let's count carefully. James has 12 book symbols. Aiden has 11 book symbols. Add those two numbers together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 28 — added all four categories instead of just James and Aiden

steps.text: Here's a hint: we only need James and Aiden, not everyone. Count James's symbols, then Aiden's symbols, then add just those two.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System counts James's row showing 12, then Aiden's row showing 11, then combines to show 23

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. First, count James's books — that's 12 symbols. Then count Aiden's books — that's 11 symbols. Now add them: 12 plus 11 equals 23 books in all. The answer is 23.
